pattern: Locate the node before the nth-from-end target with a two-pointer gap of n, then splice that target out in a single forward pass.
hints: Counting from the end is awkward going forward — instead keep two pointers separated by exactly n nodes so that when the leading one reaches the end, the trailing one sits right where you need it.
Advance a lead pointer n steps first; then move lead and a trailing pointer together until lead falls off the end. The trailing pointer now stops just before the node to delete.
Use a dummy node in front of the head so deleting the very first node is handled the same way; after positioning, set prev.next = prev.next.next and return dummy.next.

edge_inputs.description: single node removed leaves an empty list
remove the head (nth-from-end equals the length)
interior removal in a longer list
remove the middle of an odd-length list

stress.description: random list at the length cap with n chosen within [1, length]
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    length = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 30)))
    vals = [rng.randint(0, 100) for _ in range(length)]
    n = rng.randint(1, length)
    return [vals, n]
